input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S5-ahc032-mod-stamp
Title: S5. AHC032 / Mod Stamp

Problem:
Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3 x 3 stamps on a 9 x 9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

Grading target:
Score is the sum of final cell values modulo 998244353.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Reformulate the problem into a complete and self-contained mathematical specification. You must ensure that every piece of input data, including all numerical values, grid entries, dimensions, and constants provided in the original text, is explicitly included in your reformulation. The goal is to create a version that allows a solver to compute the exact final answer without needing to refer back to the original source.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S5-ahc032-mod-stamp
Title: S5. AHC032 / Mod Stamp

Problem:
Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3 x 3 stamps on a 9 x 9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

Grading target:
Score is the sum of final cell values modulo 998244353.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
The user wants a complete mathematical specification of the provided problem. Since the original problem text is missing the actual 9x9 grid values, I must represent the problem as a template where the grid A is a parameter, but I will note that the specific values were not provided in the prompt. However, looking at the prompt again, it seems the user provided the structure of a question but omitted the actual grid data. I will formulate the mathematical model assuming a grid A exists.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Maximize the sum of board entries after applying up to K=81 stamp placements on a 9x9 grid. Mathematical Specification: 1. Input Data: A 9x9 grid of integers A, where A[i,j] represents the value at row i and column j (1 <= i, j <= 9). A stamp size of 3x3. A maximum number of placements K = 81. A modulus M = 998244353. 2. Decision Variables: Let S be a set of top-left coordinates (r, c) for the 3x3 stamps. For each (r, c) in S, the stamp covers cells (i, j) such that r <= i < r+3 and c <= j < c+3. Each placement of a 3x3 stamp at (r, c) (where 1 <= r, c <= 7) adds the sum of the 3x3 area to the total sum. If multiple stamps overlap, the values are added multiple times. 3. Objective Function: Maximize Z = (sum over (r, c) in S of (sum over i=r to r+2 and j=c to c+2 of A[i,j])) mod 998244353.
